name: Subjunctive Intro — espero que, quiero que

cards.front: What triggers the present subjunctive in Spanish?
cards.back: Emotion verbs and wish verbs in the main clause, followed by 'que'. Example: espero que, quiero que, me alegra que.
cards.grammarNote: The subjunctive is a mood (not a tense) used when the main clause expresses a wish, emotion, doubt, or uncertainty about what happens in the subordinate clause.

cards.front: How do you form the present subjunctive?
cards.back: Take the yo present → drop -o → add opposite vowel endings. -AR → -e endings (hable). -ER/-IR → -a endings (coma, abra).
cards.grammarNote: Examples: hablar → hablo → habl- → hable/hables/hable/hablemos/hablen. Comer → como → com- → coma/comas/coma/comamos/coman.
Espero que triggers the subjunctive: 'te sientas' (subjunctive of sentirse). Note: sentir is a stem-changing verb, so the stem change applies in the subjunctive too: sient-.

cards.front: I want you to be calm.
cards.grammarNote: Quiero que (I want you/him to) always triggers the subjunctive when the subjects are different. 'Estés' is the subjunctive of estar.

cards.front: I'm afraid it might be serious.
cards.back: Tengo miedo de que sea grave.
cards.grammarNote: Sea is the subjunctive of ser. 'Tengo miedo de que' = I'm afraid that. The de connects the main clause to the subordinate clause.
Ojalá comes from Arabic 'law sha Allah' (may God wish). It always triggers the subjunctive and is one of the most common subjunctive triggers. The 'que' is optional: '¡Ojalá salga bien!'

cards.front: It's good that you talk with him.
cards.back: Es bueno que hables con él.

cards.front: I want to rest. (same subject — no subjunctive)
cards.grammarNote: Key rule: if the subject is the same in both clauses, use an infinitive, not the subjunctive. 'Quiero descansar' (I want to rest = same person). 'Quiero que descanses' (I want YOU to rest = different person → subjunctive).

cards.front: I want you to rest. (different subject — subjunctive)
I hope he feels better soon.
cards.back: Espero que se sienta mejor pronto.
